tweets.text:
Yet another life lost in a sanctuary county at the hands of a criminal alien who should have never been here in the first place.

Fairfax County PD arrested Chavarria Muy of Guatemala in connection to a fatal stabbing of a man in Bailey’s Crossroads, Virginia. The male victim was pronounced dead at the scene.

When will sanctuary politicians wake up and begin putting American lives FIRST?

tweets.text:
“On April 7, 2026, ICE officers were conducting a targeted vehicle stop in Patterson, CA to arrest Carlos Ivan Mendoza Hernandez, an 18th Street Gang member wanted in El Salvador for questioning in connection to a murder. As officers approached the car, the wanted gang member weaponized his vehicle in an attempt to run an officer over. Following their training, our officers fired defensive shots to protect themselves, their fellow agents, and the public.

“The illegal alien was taken to a local hospital.

“The FBI is on the scene. This is a developing situation and we will update the public when more information is available.”

— ICE Director Todd M. Lyons
tweets.text_resolved:

Following their training, our officers fired defensive shots to protect themselves, their fellow agents, and the public.

“The illegal alien was taken to a local hospital.

“The FBI is on the scene. This is a developing situation and we will update the public when more information is available.”

tweets.text:
Jocelyn Nungaray should be a teenager now.

Instead, she was sexually assaulted and brutally murdered in June 2024 by two Venezuelan nationals in the country illegally.

Thanks to ICE’s VOICE Office, immigration crime victims and their families are not alone.

Our skilled liaisons:
 
—Help victims understand the immigration enforcement and removal process
—Provide victims with access to social service professionals and resources
—Help victims receive custody status updates on criminal aliens 

Call 855-48-VOICE, Monday-Friday 8 A.M. to 8 P.M. ET or visit us online at https://t.co/Eh8BbBROsn.

Celebrating the one-year anniversary on April 9 of the Trump Administration's VOICE Office relaunch.

tweets.text:
Carissa Aspnes was seriously and tragically injured in a hit-and-run accident caused by an illegal alien who should never have been in our country, let alone behind the wheel.

ICE deported the illegal alien who robbed Carissa of her dreams and her voice. And ICE’s VOICE Office will aways stand behind her and her family.

ICE thanks Patricia Fox, Carissa’s mother, for the strength she showed today telling her daughter’s story.

tweets.text:
Megan Bos’ future was erased by an illegal alien who had no right to be in the U.S.
 
Today marks the one-year anniversary of the day authorities found Megan’s body — covered in bleach in a Chicago suburb dumpster. Horrific.
 
ICE thanks Jennifer Bos, Megan’s mother, for the tremendous courage she showed Thursday while sharing her story of unbearable loss.
 
Thanks to the ICE VOICE office, Megan will never be forgotten, and Jennifer will never be alone.

tweets.text:
Minh Duy Tong Nguyen aka Tony Playboy, of Vietnam, was once a fugitive wanted for the murder of two people and the attempted murder of three others. He was featured on an episode of "America’s Most Wanted" in 1990 and subsequently captured in Canada and extradited to the U.S. to stand trial for murder. 

He was sentenced to 45-years in the state prison.

ICE @EROHouston took custody of him April 16, upon his release from prison.

His deportation order is over 25 years old.

He’s about to go home.

tweets.text:
ICE HSI and its partners repatriated 26 ancient artifacts to the Hellenic Republic of Greece during a ceremony at the Embassy of Greece in Washington, D.C. April 23.

This marble torso of Asclepius, ancient Greek god of medicine and healing from the 1st or 2nd century, which stands 40 inches tall, was among the repatriated artifacts. Law enforcement seized it following an HSI and CBP investigation.

🔎See more repatriated objects and learn more in our newsroom: https://t.co/5vn8PyNdsU

tweets.text:
Nhan Tu Hoang, of Vietnam, killed Kathy Brown Arceneaux, at her home Feb. 29, 1996.

⚖️Court documents tell the horrific, tragic story:

➡️Hoang and some friends had been out drinking and decided to rob houses. They grabbed a rifle and Hoang proceeded to fire shots at a house. When dogs began barking, Hoang and his friend shot at the animals, then left the scene to reload the firearm.  

➡️Hoang testified that when he later returned to scene, he was startled by a woman leaning over her dog and started shooting.

➡️Hoang shot Arceneaux five times — killing her. 

He was convicted and sentenced to prison. ICE arrested him May 5 upon his release and will hold him pending deportation.
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Zakaria Songotoua was convicted in absentia and sentenced to 30 years for murder, assault and battery after he fled Burkina Faso before trial. 

As a member of the Presidential Security Regiment, he participated in the attempted 2015 coup d’etat in the African nation that included killing 11 and holding the interim president hostage.

After hiding in the sanctuary city of Bronx, N.Y., our New York officers and special agents tracked him down and returned him to Burkina Faso to face justice.

The U.S. is no safe haven for war criminals, and ICE HSI's Human Rights Violators and War Crimes Center ensures those who engage in such bloodshed anywhere in the world have no place in the United States.

tweets.text:
Sanctuary politicians are choosing criminal illegal aliens over public safety by denying undercover license plates and access to driver and vehicle registration information for ICE and CBP.

This reckless policy choice impacts our efforts to investigate cases including:
• Child exploitation
• Rape & kidnapping
• Drug and weapons trafficking
• Sex and forced labor trafficking
• Transnational criminal organizations

Thank you @TheJusticeDept for taking action.

• Transnational criminal organizations

Thank you @TheJusticeDept for taking action.

author.description: Protects America through criminal investigations and by enforcing immigration law to preserve national security and public safety.  https://t.co/rDI2IeFofc

tweets.text:
Oscar Alberto Pelaez was born in Colombia and immigrated to the U.S. to serve as a priest in California.

➡️1999 | Pelaez submitted an application for naturalization, claiming he never committed a crime for which he had not been arrested. 

➡️2001 | Pelaez became a U.S. citizen. 

➡️2002 | Pelaez was convicted of 13 felony offenses after confessing to repeatedly raping a 14-year-old boy in his parish between 1998 and 2000. 

Thanks to an ICE-initiated case through Operation False Haven, the Department of Justice filed a civil complaint to revoke Pelaez’s citizenship for concealing these crimes while applying for naturalization.
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Despite his 2014 cocaine trafficking conviction and a 2018 final removal order, Cuban illegal alien Pedro Fernandez thought he’d casually catch a flight from Florida to Chicago.

ICE and its Florida TSA and Palm Beach Sheriff’s Office partners thought differently. 

They teamed up to arrest him in a targeted immigration enforcement operation at Palm Beach International Airport May 7, and he’ll soon be deported.

👟In 2018, Antonio Efrain Morales-Diego, of Guatemala, told Border Patrol agents that he snuck into the U.S. on foot near San Luis, Ariz. 

➡️Border Patrol released him on an order of recognizance and issued him a notice to appear.

Morales-Diego made his way to Virginia where he was later convicted of abduction and strangulation.

⚖️INA § 237(a)(2)(A)(iii): Alien convicted of an aggravated felony

author.description: Protects America through criminal investigations and by enforcing immigration law to preserve national security and public safety.  https://t.co/rDI2IeFofc

tweets.text:
Miguel Solis-Martinez, of Honduras, was arrested in 2008 for soliciting someone to murder his spouse in Bexar County, Texas. He was sentenced to a decade behind bars.

🔎Solis snuck into the country in 1999 near San Ysidro, Calif., and was put into removal proceedings. An immigration judge granted him voluntary departure in 2002 — in other words, he agreed to leave the country on his own rather than get deported. He didn’t leave though.

In addition to the homicide-related conviction, his rap sheet also includes:
➡️Illegal re-entry conviction
➡️Forgery charge
➡️DUI arrest
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Illegal alien Wenceslao O. Panameno was deported back to El Salvador by ICE in 2017 following a DUI and five-year sentence for RAPING A CHILD in Massachusetts.

Then, a 2025 ICE tipline call led our Boston officers and HSI special agents to a residence where they reintroduced themselves to Panameno, who says he snuck in using a passport with someone else’s name. 

He’s now back in our custody following a year in prison for illegal reentry and will be deported soonest.

📞Your tips make America safer: (866) 347-2423.

tweets.text:
@ICEgov & ICE @HSI_HQ will be working with local and federal partners to secure the @FIFAWorldCup.

They will play a pivotal role in combatting human trafficking as well as stopping counterfeit merchandise and counterfeit ticket sales. https://t.co/KNcxnZdaSx
